schema: one record per retried or replayable operation, per Cycle-3 brief section 6

idempotency_taxonomy.true_idempotent: replay produces the identical durable state AND the identical external state
idempotency_taxonomy.content_addressed_dedup: the id is a hash of the content, so a replay collides and no-ops
idempotency_taxonomy.first_write_wins: setdefault semantics — the first writer's value survives
idempotency_taxonomy.last_write_wins: upsert semantics — a later writer overwrites
idempotency_taxonomy.retry_suppressed: deliberately NOT retried because the remote result is ambiguous
idempotency_taxonomy.accidental_repeatability: repeatable only by luck of the current code shape, not by contract
idempotency_taxonomy.not_idempotent: a replay produces a second external effect

core_property: Every entity id is content-addressed (ids.py:7, SHA-1 truncated to 12 hex, \x00-joined, usedforsecurity=False; builtin hash() is BANNED because PEP 456 salts it per-interpreter). Combined with setdefault in the add_* family, a WHOLE-PIPELINE REPLAY IS A NO-OP for entity creation. This is the system's central idempotency property and it holds.

operations.idempotency_id: NONE — the backend has NO idempotency key (models.py:118-121)
retryable: ConnectTimeout (connection NEVER established => body never sent)
operations.duplicate_side_effect_risk: NONE. The two retried classes are both PRE-SEND. Every possibly-sent class parks in needs_reconcile and is NEVER re-POSTed.
operations.verdict: SOUND. This is the system's best-engineered failure surface.

operations.sibling_parity: VERIFIED BYTE-FOR-BYTE SYMMETRIC with R-01. Checked specifically because src/fanops/CLAUDE.md warns that sibling parity is where the bugs live. It holds here.

operations.what_it_actually_retries: ONLY _ensure_media (the media UPLOAD). It CANNOT retry the publish POST, because poster.publish never lets a transient escape — it converts every one to a returned ledger. The ONLY exception that escapes poster.publish is AuthError, and _is_fatal_auth_error re-raises it FIRST (run.py:320).

duplicate_side_effect_risk.r2: NONE — the object key is fanops/{sha256(file)[:32]}{suffix} (postiz.py:171), CONTENT-ADDRESSED, so a re-upload overwrites the same object.
duplicate_side_effect_risk.postiz_multipart: A retry creates a SECOND media object in Postiz storage (postiz.py:242 is not content-addressed). An orphaned-media leak, NOT a double-publish.

operations.verdict: SOUND for double-publish. Minor media leak on the non-R2 path.

operations.idempotency_id: the counter lives INSIDE Post.error_reason as 'transient_daemon_retry=n/3' (COUP-03)
operations.clears: submission_id = None (run.py:423) — so a re-queued post can never hit the skip-resubmit strand
operations.terminal_state: after 3 cycles the post stays terminal `failed`
operations.known_defect: C3-F3 — `except Exception: return requeued` (run.py:428-429) is the ONLY handler in post/run.py with NO log line, and `requeued` is incremented INSIDE the txn, so a commit failure returns a NON-ZERO count while nothing persisted. Blast radius NIL today: publish_due discards the return value.
operations.verdict: BOUNDED and correct. Observability gap only.

terminal_state.fake_fanops_token_on_POSTIZ: submitting --24h--> needs_reconcile --72h--> 'GAVE UP:' (both ages measured from scheduled_time, NOT chained)
terminal_state.fake_token_on_ZERNIO: *** NONE *** — C3-F2. ZernioStatusClient.get_status RAISES RuntimeError on a 404; the escalation (reconcile.py:746) and give-up (reconcile.py:757) sit on the SUCCESSFUL-POLL branch (reconcile.py:739). A raising poll is caught at :627 and `continue`s at :635 — it can NEVER reach them. PostizStatusClient returns {'status':'unknown'} instead of raising, which is the ONLY reason the machinery works at all.
terminal_state.REAL_submission_id_on_EITHER_backend: *** NONE *** — C3-F1. _is_fake_token (reconcile.py:77) gates BOTH terminals. A real-token post gets ONE 'stuck ...' breadcrumb, then reconcile.py:767 (`if post.error_reason: continue`) silently skips it on every later pass. FOREVER.

operations.executed_proof: EXP-4: a fanops_ post on the Zernio shape held `submitting` at +6h, +25h, +73h, +1000h and +100000h. A real-token post on the WORKING (Postiz) shape did the same.
operations.manual_recovery: TWO-STEP and undiscoverable: `fanops resolve <id> failed` (clears the marker) then Studio recover_posts (failed->queued). The Studio requeue family accepts ONLY failed/error, so a `submitting` post is not offered at all.

operations.trigger: a queued post that already carries a REAL submission_id
operations.behaviour: CLAIM flips it queued->submitting and COMMITS (run.py:272). The NETWORK phase then SKIPS the POST entirely (logs `skip_resubmit_existing_id`). FINALIZE persists `submitting` (run.py:355-357). _publish_one returns the string 'submitting' — a SUCCESS-SHAPED value for an operation that did nothing.
operations.how_a_real_sid_re_enters_queued: bulk_send_to_review (studio/actions.py) DELIBERATELY preserves submission_id ('Don't touch submission_id / batch_id — keep the lineage'), and Ledger.approve_post (ledger.py:591) does not clear it either. HYPOTHESIS TESTED AND DISCONFIRMED: all five requeue paths (run.py:423, actions.py:1001/1032/1063/1104) DO clear it.
operations.classification: not_idempotent (it is a no-op that LOOKS like a publish)
operations.terminal_state: NONE — feeds directly into R-05's C3-F1 hole
operations.verdict: C3-F1. The intent (MOL-115: never double-POST) is correct; the CLAIM should not have happened.

operations.one_deliberate_exception: _mint_surface_post DELETES and re-mints a post whose prior state is `rejected` or `failed` (crosspost.py:229-231) — so a re-run CAN resurrect a rejected post.

operations.idempotency_id: an EPOCH-SUFFIXED id — a NEW id on every click
operations.rationale: Project hard rule: 'fan accounts repost freely; NEVER build supersede/no-double-post.' Double-clicking Repost mints TWO awaiting_approval posts. Both require approval before they can publish, so no auto-publish risk.

operations.replay_rule: Ledger.already_seen(sha256=...) is the explicit dedup read. Re-ingesting the same bytes is a NO-OP.

operations.idempotency_id: the render fingerprint. geom = bool(track) or (focus is not None and len(focus) > 2) (clip.py:633); content_type + _REFRAME_GEOM_V (=4) are hashed ONLY when geom is true, so centred clips keep their historic fingerprint and never needlessly re-render.
operations.atomicity: ffmpeg -> <dst>.part.mp4 -> os.replace. The .mp4 SUFFIX IS REQUIRED (it picks the muxer, MOL-78).

operations.idempotency_id: request_id, stamped into the request and echoed by the response

terminal_state.moment_hooks / captions: a SYNTHESISED clean fail-open response so ingest proceeds

operations.stale_answer_guard: _answer_one re-reads latest_request_id after the model call and DROPS the answer if the gate re-seeded mid-call (responder.py:115-119). read_response also compares request_id (agentstep.py:101).
operations.known_defect: C3-F6 — the attempt COUNTER (agentstep.bump_attempts, agentstep.py:147) uses a bare write_text, NOT the tmp+os.replace its two siblings in the same file use. A torn attempts.json hits `except Exception: n = 0` (:144-145) and SILENTLY RESETS THE CEILING, making the bounded 3-attempt escalation UNBOUNDED. Proven by EXP-7.
operations.verdict: BOUNDED in the happy path; the bound is defeated by a torn control file.

operations.classification: true_idempotent (a pure read; the gate response is written once, atomically)

operations.crash_recovery: the flock is KERNEL-RELEASED on process death — kill -9 self-heals with no manual rm

operations.retry_cadence: time.sleep(interval). NO jitter. NO backoff on repeated failure.
operations.behaviour_on_failure: `except Exception as e: print(f'run halted: ...', file=sys.stderr)` then sleep and RE-DRIVE. _cmd_run_pass already catches everything and returns None (cli.py:947-949).
operations.consequence: A persistent AuthError (a rotated Postiz key) is re-driven EVERY TICK, FOREVER, with NO heartbeat written (the heartbeat is emitted only when s is not None, cli.py:1306) and NO ledger state change.
operations.known_defect: C3-F8 (observability) + C3-F9 — daemon.status still reports verdict `alive`, because daemon_progress treats 'the newest run.log line of ANY kind' as liveness and a failing pass still writes stage lines.
operations.verdict: The INV-11 property (never burn the queue) HOLDS. What does NOT hold is any implication that the run STOPS.

operations.guard: written ONLY when currently unset (`not c.media_url`) — first-writer-wins, no clobber
operations.invalidation_rule: *** NONE ***. If the hosted URL expires, nothing invalidates the cache — a dead CDN URL is reused forever. FALSIFIES brief section 9 claim #9 ('every filesystem cache has an invalidation rule').

operations.exclusion: fcntl.flock on 00_control/ledger.lock — MUTUALLY INVISIBLE to the SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE every other writer uses
operations.duplicate_side_effect_risk: SILENT DATA LOSS. Cycle 2 PROVED by execution that a live writer holding BEGIN IMMEDIATE had the db file os.replace'd out from under it; its commit() SUCCEEDED with no exception and its data was silently discarded.
operations.exposure: NO production caller (operator break-glass), but ledger_wipe.py:246 advertises it as THE wipe rollback path — so an operator following the DOCUMENTED recovery procedure while the daemon is running hits this.
operations.verdict: F-B (Cycle 2), upheld. Cycle 3 did NOT re-run this destructive path.

adversarial_verdicts.claim_5_every_external_side_effect_is_id: UPHELD for the publish POST (claim-before-network + park-never-re-POST). PARTIAL for uploads (R2 yes, Postiz multipart no).
